name: Numbers to 999 — Hundreds, Tens and Ones
whatIsIt: After 99 comes a brand-new bundle: a HUNDRED (ten tens make one hundred). Big numbers up to 999 are built from three kinds of bundles — HUNDREDS, TENS and ONES. In 235 there are 2 hundreds (200), 3 tens (30) and 5 ones (5): 200 + 30 + 5 = 235. Each digit's PLACE tells you which bundle it counts.
whyNeeded: Real life quickly goes past 100 — 250 rupees, 365 days, house number 428, 500 grams. To read, write, say and compare these numbers, a child needs the hundreds place. It's the bridge from counting to 100 into all bigger numbers.
realLifeUses: Pages in a storybook or steps counted on a walk

vocabulary.meaning: What a digit is worth by WHERE it sits: hundreds, tens or ones.
A number from 100 to 999 with a hundreds, tens and ones digit.
Writing a number as its bundles: 235 = 200 + 30 + 5.

story.title: The Big Bead Jar
story.text: Kabir's class strings beads into tens. When they get ten tens, Teacher says, 'That whole bundle is ONE HUNDRED!' They make 2 hundred-bundles, 3 ten-sticks, and 5 loose beads. 'How many beads in all?' asks Teacher. Kabir reads the places: 2 hundreds is 200, 3 tens is 30, 5 ones is 5. '200 + 30 + 5 = 235 beads!'
story.extractedProblem: 2 hundreds, 3 tens and 5 ones — what number is that?

visual.caption: 235 built from blocks: 2 hundred-flats, 3 ten-rods, 5 one-cubes. The place of each digit tells which bundle it counts.

teachingGallery.title: Ten tens make a hundred
teachingGallery.note: Bundle ten ten-rods and you get one hundred-flat.

examples.caption: One hundred is ten ten-rods bundled together.
342: the 3 is worth 300, the 4 is worth 40, the 2 is worth 2.
999 is the largest three-digit number; one more makes 1000.

standardMethod.summary: Split a number into hundreds, tens and ones. The hundreds digit counts hundred-bundles, the tens digit counts ten-bundles, the ones digit counts loose ones. Add the bundle values.
steps: Look at the LAST digit — that is the ones.
The MIDDLE digit counts tens (its value ends in 0).
The FIRST digit counts hundreds (its value ends in 00).
Ten tens always bundle into one hundred; after 199 comes 200.

workedExamples.problem: What is the value of the 3 in 342?
steps: The 3 is the FIRST digit — the hundreds place.
199 is 1 hundred, 9 tens, 9 ones — the ones are full.
Add one: the tens fill up too, so they all bundle up.

commonMistakes.mistake: Saying the 2 in 235 is worth just 2 instead of 200.
commonMistakes.fix: A digit's worth depends on its PLACE. The 2 sits in the hundreds place, so it is worth 200, not 2.

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinking the number after 199 is something like 1900 or 100-again.
commonMistakes.fix: When ones and tens are both full (199), everything bundles up one more hundred → 200.

easy.q: How many tens make one hundred?
In 235, what is the ONES digit?

medium.q: What is the value of the 3 in 342? (hundreds place)

challenge.q: In 428, how much is the 4 worth?

options.label: 400 — it is in the hundreds place

challenge.answer: 400 — it is in the hundreds place

hintLadder: Fill every place with the biggest digit.

teachBackPrompt: Use bundles (straws or beans in tens) to show a grown-up that ten tens make one hundred. Build 235 with hundreds, tens and ones, and say what each digit is worth: 200, 30, 5.

revisionCard.summary: After 99 comes hundreds (ten tens = 100). A 3-digit number has hundreds, tens and ones places. 235 = 200 + 30 + 5. A digit's worth depends on its PLACE (the 2 in 235 = 200). After 199 comes 200. The biggest 3-digit number is 999.
